resources.string: Pause this session before deleting it.
Pause this session before editing an earlier message.
This session is no longer running. Try sending again.
Finish editing before compacting this conversation.
There is no conversation to compact yet.
Pause this session before compacting it.
There is not enough conversation to compact yet.
There is no text to compact.
Aether %1$s is available. You can download the APK and install it now.
Unable to open the APK installer
Sending will replace the replies that came after it.
Virtual display preview will appear after the agent starts.
Follow the system light or dark appearance.
Use a darker interface across the app.
Choose whether each session gets its own workspace or everyone shares one workspace.
Injected into every Termux bash command, for example HTTP_PROXY or HTTPS_PROXY.
Termux command access is already working. You do not need to run Root automatic setup again.
Aether runs bash through Termux. Finish setup here so tool calls work for every user without manual adb steps.
Permission is granted and the setup probe succeeded.
All sessions start in ~/.aether/workspace and uploads are kept under uploads/.
Each session keeps using its own directory under ~/.aether/workspaces/.
Aether can silently wake Termux in the background with Root when Termux is not already running.
Aether can use su to enable Termux command access and background wake-up.
On rooted devices, this avoids the manual Termux permission and settings flow.
Retry after granting su to Aether, or continue with the manual setup actions below.
Manage MCP servers, inspect each transport config, and keep only the connections you want active.
Add HTTP or stdio servers to extend capabilities.
Update the transport config and quick action source for this MCP server.
Add an HTTP server for remote APIs or a stdio server for local Termux processes.
Optional headers, one KEY=VALUE per line.
Optional environment variables, one KEY=VALUE per line.
Authorize isolated virtual-display tools with Shizuku or Root. Skip this on devices without either option.
Enables isolated virtual-display tools. Requires Shizuku or Root.
Uses root shell for privileged input.
Shizuku mode creates the display from a Shizuku user service so apps can render off the main screen. Root mode remains experimental.
No Agent Mode virtual display is active.
No displays are currently visible to Aether.
No su binary was detected on this device.
Grant su to Aether, then refresh this status.
Root authorization was denied. Grant su to Aether, then refresh this status.
Refresh Root status before using Agent Mode.
Root mode will request su when Agent Mode starts.
Shizuku is running and Aether is authorized.
Install Shizuku before using Shizuku mode.
Start Shizuku, then refresh this status.
Grant Aether permission in Shizuku before using Agent Mode.
Shizuku permission was denied. Request it again or enable Aether inside Shizuku.
Save Agent Mode settings, then refresh Shizuku status.
Switch to Root mode to inspect Root status.
Complete Termux setup before configuring Agent Mode.
Detect su and wait for authorization.
Enable external apps and grant command permission.
Select Root authorization for local device control.
Import or export the complete local Aether data set as JSON.
Export recent Aether logcat output and diagnostic metadata.
Fetch the latest GitHub Release and show the update prompt even when the installed version is current.
Testing switch for Agent Mode gating. On treats Termux as ready; off treats it as not ready.
Starts from Termux, then goes through Agent Mode, Tavily, Skills, and MCP.
Check GitHub Releases for a newer APK
Aether is requesting su, enabling Termux command access, and preparing Root Agent Mode.
Termux command access and Root Agent Mode authorization are ready.
Start setup to grant su and finish the local access configuration.
No su binary was detected on this device.
Grant su to Aether, then try Root automatic setup again.
Install Termux first, then return to this setup.
The setup command finished with an error. Review the detail above and retry when ready.
Configure Termux first. Agent Mode uses the Termux bridge for its workspace and screenshot flow, so authorization cannot be enabled until Termux is ready.
Root Agent Mode authorization is already ready. You do not need to run Root automatic setup again.
Agent Mode authorization is already ready. Root automatic setup is not required; continuing will reconfigure Agent Mode to Root.
Use su to select Root mode and prepare local device control automatically.
URL fetch ready, Tavily not configured
Replay the first-run landing and setup flow
Choose dedicated defaults for chat, titles, naming, and compaction.
Used for new chats and when a conversation has not selected a model yet.
Used when Aether automatically generates conversation titles.
Used for automatic naming flows such as Agent Skills and MCP labels.
Used when /compact summarizes the current conversation.
Add a provider to connect to an LLM API.
Enable at least one provider model first, then return here to choose a default.
fetch_web_url works without extra setup. tavily_search uses this API key and Base URL; leave the URL blank to use the official Tavily endpoint.
Uses an Android foreground service so active chats can keep working after you leave Aether.
Shows a completion alert when a run ends while Aether is not on screen.
If a request produces no response activity at all for this many seconds, Aether cancels that attempt and reconnects with backoff. Range: 30-3600 seconds.
Manage installed skills and keep only the bundles you want Aether to use in chat.
Import skills from a folder, zip, or remote URL.
Import Agent Skills from a local folder, zip file, or remote URL.
GitHub repo/tree URLs and direct zip links are supported.
Aether wakes at the next matching time and runs the task prompt as an automated Agent turn.
Create one manually, or ask the Agent to create one with its scheduling tool.
Disabled tasks stay saved but do not wake Aether.
Interval windows are optional. Leave start and end blank to run all day.
Use lowercase letters, numbers, and underscores only.
This provider ID is already in use.
Use Vertex AI Express Mode generateContent.
Compatibility mode uses basic function calling and disables parallel or batch tool features for providers with limited tool support.
Enabled models appear in the chat model picker. This request format requires an API key.
Enabled models appear in the chat model picker. Refresh loads models from the provider API.
Preview unavailable for this file type.
Couldn\'t read image data from the workspace.
Aether uses Termux to run shell commands on-device.
Allow Aether to use the "Run commands in Termux environment" Android permission.
Termux seems to be not running in the background
Open Termux and keep it running in the background, then return to Aether and refresh.
Open Termux, paste the Aether setup command, then return and refresh.
Open Termux once and verify its integration settings.
If this phone has Root, Aether can configure Termux and Agent Mode automatically.
Root is available. Grant su to Aether to skip the manual Termux and Agent Mode steps.
Aether is enabling Termux external apps, granting command permission, and selecting Root Agent Mode.
Root setup is complete. Termux and Agent Mode are ready.
If you have Root, try the automatic setup. Otherwise continue with the existing manual flow.
Root access was denied or timed out.
Install Termux first, then Aether can finish this setup with Root.
Root setup did not complete. You can retry or continue manually.
Install Termux first, then come back here.
Grant the "Run commands in Termux environment" permission in Android settings.
Open Termux once, then return and refresh.
Copy the setup command, paste it in Termux, then return and refresh.
Open Termux once, then refresh here.
Copy the setup command, paste it in Termux, then return and refresh.
The on-device agent that works with everything.
Later, you can add Skills so Aether remembers reusable workflows you like.
You do not need this now.
Use them for prompts, checks, and templates.
If you want live docs, search, or APIs later, you can connect MCP servers in Settings.
You can leave this for later.
This is where Aether grows beyond local tools.
First, let\'s choose your model provider.
Great. Add your key and base URL. I\'ll fetch the models after this.
Pick the model you want, and then we can go straight into chat.
You can change this later in Settings.
I\'ll place the best matches first when I can find them.
Great. Now let’s give Aether access to your device so tools can run locally.
Agent Mode is optional. It needs Root or Shizuku to control an isolated Android display.
Choose an authorization method, or skip this for now.
Use the elevated Shizuku service when the app is installed.
Use a root shell for privileged input on rooted devices.
Leave Agent Mode off and enable it later from Settings.
If you want fresher web answers later, you can add search here.
This is optional. URL fetch already works without it.
